COOP Stewardship Agreement
Post-Close Engagement
Seller
{{sellerName}}
Company
{{companyName}}
Date
{{sentDate}}
Reference
{{ref}}
§ 01Engagement Overview

This COOP Stewardship Agreement establishes the terms under which Forhemit Stewardship Management Co. will provide post-close operational support to {{companyName}} following the completion of the ESOP transaction. The engagement begins on {{startDate}} and continues for a period of {{engagementTerm}} months, concluding on {{endDate}}.

The purpose of this engagement is to ensure continuity and stability as {{companyName}} transitions to employee ownership. Forhemit will serve as an embedded advisor across four operational tracks, helping the company build the systems, culture, and governance structures necessary for long-term ESOP success.

§ 02Four Tracks

The stewardship program is organized into four tracks, each addressing a critical aspect of the employee ownership transition:

T1People. Leadership development, key person retention strategies, employee communication plans, and ownership culture cultivation. Includes quarterly leadership workshops and employee engagement surveys.
T2Systems. Process documentation, technology upgrades, operational resilience improvements, and knowledge transfer protocols. Focus on reducing key-person dependencies and building institutional knowledge.
T3Financial. Reporting cadence establishment, covenant compliance monitoring, debt service management, and ESOP administration support. Includes monthly financial review and lender communication.
T4Governance. Board development, ESOP committee formation, fiduciary compliance, and regulatory reporting. Includes annual governance assessment and trustee coordination.
§ 03Deliverables

Forhemit will provide the following deliverables throughout the engagement:

A.Monthly Check-Ins. Scheduled calls to review progress across all four tracks, address challenges, and adjust priorities as needed.
B.Quarterly Reports. Written progress reports delivered to the seller and ESOP trustee, covering milestones achieved, open items, and recommendations.
C.Annual Review. Comprehensive annual assessment of all four tracks with updated stewardship plan for the following year (if engagement is renewed).
D.Gate Milestones. Targeted deliverables aligned with key milestones in the post-close timeline, including refinance readiness (Month 14-18) and seller note retirement confirmation.
§ 04Fee Structure
{{annualFee}} (2.5% of QofE EBITDA)
{{ebitdaTTM}}
{{quarterlyFee}}
Invoiced at the start of each quarter
Net 30 days
Note: The stewardship fee is a tax-deductible business expense. It is separate from and in addition to any transaction fees paid during the closing process. Fees are reviewed annually and adjusted based on updated EBITDA.
§ 05Term & Renewal
12 months from {{startDate}}
Auto-renewal for an additional 12 months unless terminated
90 days written notice by either party
{{engagementTerm}} months

Either party may elect not to renew at the end of any 12-month period by providing written notice at least 90 days prior to the renewal date. The engagement will conclude no later than {{endDate}} regardless of renewal status.

§ 06Roles & Responsibilities
A.Forhemit's Role. Serve as post-close stewardship advisor. Provide strategic guidance, operational support, and accountability across all four tracks. Coordinate with the ESOP trustee, lender, and other advisors as needed.
B.Company's Role. Designate a primary point of contact for day-to-day coordination. Implement recommendations in a timely manner. Provide access to financial records, operational data, and personnel as needed for stewardship activities.
C.Reporting Structure. Forhemit reports to the seller (or designated board representative). Quarterly reports are shared with the ESOP trustee. Forhemit does not have decision-making authority — all decisions remain with the company's management and board.
§ 07Termination

Either party may terminate this agreement with 90 days written notice. Upon termination:

A.Prorated refund. If the company terminates mid-quarter, any prepaid fees for the remaining portion of the quarter will be refunded on a prorated basis.
B.Transition support. Forhemit will provide a 30-day transition period to hand off active work items, introduce replacement advisors (if requested), and deliver a final stewardship summary.
C.Confidentiality. All confidentiality obligations survive termination. Forhemit will not disclose any company information obtained during the engagement.
§ 08Contact

For questions about this agreement, please contact:

deals@forhemit.com
